rules.name: Patient Hunter
rules.effect: During the third, fourth, and fifth battle rounds, ranged weapons equipped by **^^T’au Empire^^** models from your army have the **[SUSTAINED HITS 1]** ability. During the third, fourth and fifth battle rounds, while a unit is a Guided unit (see For the Greater Good), each time a ranged attack is made by a model in that unit that targets a Spotted unit, you can ignore any or all modifiers to that attack’s Ballistic skill characteristics and/or all modifiers to the Hit roll.

rules.name: Sustained Hits
rules.effect: Weapons with **[SUSTAINED HITS X]** in their profile are known as Sustained Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if a Critical Hit is rolled, that attack scores a number of additional hits on the target as denoted by ‘x’

stratagems.name: A Tempting Trap
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. The first time you use this Stratagem, you must also select one objective marker that is not in your opponent’s deployment zone; until the end of the battle, this becomes your Trap objective marker.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack that targets an enemy unit within range of your Trap objective marker, add 1 to the Wound roll.
stratagems.summary: +1 to Wound rolls against units within Trap objective marker range

stratagems.name: Point-blank Ambush
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack that targets an enemy unit within 9", improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.
stratagems.summary: Improve AP by 1 on ranged attacks against targets within 9"

stratagems.name: Coordinate to Engage
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has just been selected as an Observer unit (see For the Greater Good).
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets their Spotted unit, improve the Ballistic Skill characteristic of that attack by 1 and, if your unit has the MARKERLIGHT keyword, that attack has the [IGNORES COVER] ability.
stratagems.summary: +1 BS vs Spotted units; [IGNORES COVER] if unit has MARKERLIGHT

stratagems.name: Combat Embarkation
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Charge phase, just after an enemy unit has declared a charge.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE INFANTRY unit from your army that was selected as one of the targets of that charge, and one friendly TRANSPORT.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can embark within that TRANSPORT. If it does, your opponent can select new targets for that charge.
stratagems.summary: Unit embarks in TRANSPORT; opponent selects new charge targets

stratagems.name: Photon Grenades
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Charge phase, just after an enemy unit has declared a charge.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE GRENADES unit from your army that was selected as one of the targets of that charge.
stratagems.effect: That enemy unit must immediately take a Battle-shock test, and until the end of the phase, subtract 2 from Charge rolls made for that enemy unit.
stratagems.summary: Enemy unit takes Battle-shock test; -2 to their Charge rolls until end of phase

stratagems.name: Wall of Mirrors
stratagems.timing: End of your opponent’s Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One STEALTH, GHOSTKEEL or COMMANDER SHADOWSUN unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Remove your unit from the battlefield and place it into Strategic Reserves.
stratagems.summary: Remove unit from battlefield; place into Strategic Reserves

enhancements.name: Exemplar of the Kauyon
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only (excluding KROOT SHAPER models). While the bearer is leading a unit, the Patient Hunter Detachment rule applies to that unit from the second battle round onwards instead of from the third.

enhancements.name: Precision of the Patient Hunter
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. Each time the bearer makes a ranged attack, add 1 to the Hit roll. From the third battle round onwards, add 1 to the Wound roll as well.

enhancements.name: Solid-image Projection Unit
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. After both players have deployed their armies, select up to three T’AU EMPIRE units from your army and redeploy them. When doing so, you can set those units up in Strategic Reserves if you wish, regardless of how many units are already in Strategic Reserves.

enhancements.name: Through Unity, Devastation
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only (excluding KROOT SHAPER models). While the bearer is leading a unit, each time that unit is an Observer unit, until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in a Guided unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability while targeting their Spotted unit.

rules.name: Killing Blow
rules.effect: During the first, second and third battle rounds, ranged weapons equipped by **^^T’au Empire^^** models from your army have the **[ASSAULT]** ability. During the first, second and third battle rounds, while a unit is a Guided unit (see For the Greater Good), its ranged weapons have the **[LETHAL HITS]** ability.

rules.name: Assault
rules.effect: Weapons with **[ASSAULT]** in their profile are known as Assault weapons. If a unit that Advanced this turn contains any models equipped with Assault weapons, it is still eligible to shoot in this turn’s Shooting phase. When such a unit is selected to shoot, you can only resolve attacks using Assault weapons its models are equipped with.

rules.name: Lethal Hits
rules.effect: Weapons with **[LETHAL HITS]** in their profile are known as Lethal Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, a Critical Hit automatically wounds the target.

stratagems.name: Pinpoint Counter-offensive
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit (excluding KROOT units) from your army that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the battle, each time a T’AU EMPIRE unit (excluding KROOT units) from your army makes an attack that targets the enemy unit that just destroyed your unit, you can re-roll the Hit roll.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Hit rolls against the unit that destroyed yours (T'AU EMPIRE only, excludes KROOT)

stratagems.name: Aggressive Mobility
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to move this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, if your unit Advances, do not make an Advance roll for it. Instead, until the end of the phase, add 6" to the Move characteristic of models in your unit.
stratagems.summary: Advancing: +6" Move instead of Advance roll

stratagems.name: Focused Fire
stratagems.cond: Two T’AU EMPIRE units from your army that have not been selected to shoot this phase, and one enemy unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in either of your units makes an attack, it can only target that enemy unit (and only if it is an eligible target for that attack), and when resolving that attack, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic by 1.
stratagems.summary: Attacks from both units target one enemy unit only; +1 AP

stratagems.name: Combat Debarkation
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE INFANTRY unit from your army that disembarked from a TRANSPORT this turn.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets the closest enemy unit, you can re-roll the Wound roll.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Wound rolls vs closest enemy unit

stratagems.name: Pulse Onslaught
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE INFANTRY unit (excluding KROOT units) from your army that has just shot, and one enemy unit (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES) hit by one or more of those attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of your opponent’s next turn, that enemy unit is shaken. While a unit is shaken, subtract 2 from its Move characteristic and subtract 2 from Advance and Charge rolls made for it.
stratagems.summary: Shaken; -2 Move, -2 Advance/Charge rolls

stratagems.name: Counterfire Defence Systems
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time an attack is allocated to your unit, subtract 1 from the Damage characteristic of that attack.
stratagems.summary: -1 Damage on all incoming attacks

enhancements.name: Coordinated Exploitation
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only (excluding KROOT SHAPER models). While the bearer is leading a unit, each time that unit is an Observer unit, until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in a Guided unit have the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability while targeting their Spotted unit.

enhancements.name: Exemplar of the Mont’ka
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only (excluding KROOT SHAPER models). While the bearer is leading a unit, the Killing Blow Detachment rule applies to that unit during the fourth battle round as well.

enhancements.name: Strategic Conqueror
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. At the start of the first battle round, before the first turn begins, select one objective marker on the battlefield. While a friendly T’AU EMPIRE model is within range of that objective marker and the bearer is on the battlefield, add 1 to that friendly model’s Objective Control characteristic.

enhancements.name: Strike Swiftly
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. At the start of the battle, before any moves are made using the Scouts ability, you can select up to two friendly T’AU EMPIRE units within 6" of the bearer that do not have the Scouts ability. Until the end of the battle, all models in the selected units have the Scouts 6" ability.

rules.name: Bonded Heroes
rules.effect: Each time a **^^T'au Empire Battlesuit^^** model from your army makes a ranged attack that targets a unit within 12", improve the Strength characteristic of that attack by 1. If that attack targets a unit within 9", improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1 as well.

stratagems.name: Fail-safe Detonator
stratagems.timing: Any phase, just after a T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT model from your army is destroyed.
stratagems.cond: That destroyed model’s unit. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even if that unit was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Before removing your model from play, if it has the Deadly Demise ability, do not roll for that ability; instead, you can choose whether the result of that roll is a 1 or a 6. If your model does not have the Deadly Demise ability, roll one D6 for each unit within 6" of it: on a 4+, that unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.
stratagems.summary: Choose Deadly Demise result (1 or 6); if no Deadly Demise, roll D6 for each unit within 6"—on 4+, D3 mortal wounds

stratagems.name: Stimm Injectors
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, models in your unit have the Feel No Pain 6+ ability.
stratagems.summary: Grant Feel No Pain 6+ to unit

stratagems.name: The Shortened Blade
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that is arriving using the Deep Strike ability this phase.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 6" horizontally away from all enemy models.
stratagems.summary: Set up unit anywhere more than 6" from enemies

stratagems.name: The Arro’kon Protocol
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit that contains 6 or more models, that attack has the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability. If that attack targets an enemy unit that contains 11 or more models, it has the [SUSTAINED HITS 2] ability instead.
stratagems.summary: [SUSTAINED HITS 1] vs units of 6+; [SUSTAINED HITS 2] vs units of 11+

stratagems.name: The Torchstar Gambit
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that can FLY whose attacks have been resolved this phase.
stratagems.effect: If your unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Normal move. If it does, your unit cannot declare a charge this turn
stratagems.summary: Move if not in Engagement Range; cannot charge this turn

stratagems.name: Grav-inhibitor Field
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Charge phase, just after an enemy unit has declared a charge.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that was selected as a target of that charge.
stratagems.effect: That enemy unit must immediately take a Battle-shock test and you must roll one D6 for each model in that enemy unit: for each 6, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.
stratagems.summary: Enemy unit takes Battle-shock test; suffer 1 mortal wound per 6 rolled on D6s equal to unit size

enhancements.name: Internal Grenade Racks
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT model only. The bearer has the Grenades keyword, and each time the bearer ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit that it moved over during that move. If you do, roll six D6: for each 4+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

enhancements.name: Prototype Weapon System
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT model only. Each time the bearer is selected to shoot, select either the [LETHAL HITS] or [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability. Until those attacks are resolved, ranged weapons equipped by the bearer have the selected ability.

enhancements.name: Puretide Engram Neurochip
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT model only. Each time you target the bearer’s unit with a Stratagem, roll one D6: on a 4+, you gain 1CP.

enhancements.name: Starflare Ignition System
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT model only. At the end of your opponent’s turn, if the bearer’s unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, you can remove that unit from the battlefield and place it into Strategic Reserves.

rules.name: Hunter's Instincts
rules.effect: Each time a **^^Kroot^^** model from your army makes an attack, add 1 to the Hit roll if the target of that attack is below its Starting Strength, and add 1 to the Wound roll as well if the target of that attack is Below Half-strength.

rules.name: Skirmish Fighters
rules.effect: **^^Kroot^^** models from your army have a 6+ invulnerable save against melee attacks and a 5+ invulnerable save against ranged attacks.

stratagems.name: Join the Hunt
stratagems.cond: One KROOT INFANTRY or KROOT HOUNDS unit from your army that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Add a new unit to your army identical to your destroyed unit, in Strategic Reserves, at its Starting Strength.
stratagems.summary: Add destroyed unit to Strategic Reserves at Starting Strength

stratagems.name: A Trap Well Laid
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: After your unit has resolved its attacks this phase, select one enemy unit that was hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the phase, each time a KROOT model from your army makes an attack that targets that enemy unit, unless the attacking unit is Battle-shocked, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.
stratagems.summary: After unit attacks, select hit enemy unit; KROOT attacks vs that unit gain +1 AP (if not Battle-shocked)

stratagems.name: Emp Grenades
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy VEHICLE unit is selected to shoot or fight.
stratagems.cond: One KROOT GRENADES unit from your army within 8" of that enemy VEHICLE unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, worsen the Weapon Skill and Ballistic Skill characteristics of that enemy VEHICLE unit’s weapons by 1.
stratagems.summary: -1 to WS and BS for target enemy VEHICLE's weapons

stratagems.name: The Grisly Feast
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that destroyed one or more enemy units this phase.
stratagems.effect: In your opponent’s next Command phase, each enemy unit within 6" of your unit must take a Battle-shock test. If the unit taking that test is Below Half-strength, subtract 1 from that test. Enemy units affected by this Stratagem do not need to take any other Battle-shock tests in the same phase.
stratagems.summary: Each enemy unit within 6" must take Battle-shock test in opponent's next Command phase; -1 if Below Half-strength. Affected units take no other Battle-shock tests that phase

stratagems.name: Guerrilla Warriors
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, just after a KROOT unit from your army Falls Back.
stratagems.cond: That KROOT unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the turn, your unit is eligible to shoot and declare a charge.
stratagems.summary: Eligible to shoot and declare charge this turn

stratagems.name: Hidden Hunters
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18".
stratagems.summary: Enemy ranged attacks only effective within 18"

stratagems.name: Join the Hunt
stratagems.cond: One KROOT INFANTRY or KROOT HOUNDS unit from your army that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that unit even though it was just destroyed.
stratagems.effect: Add a new unit to your army identical to your destroyed unit, in Strategic Reserves, at its Starting Strength.
stratagems.summary: Add destroyed unit to Strategic Reserves at Starting Strength

stratagems.name: Hidden Hunters
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18".
stratagems.summary: Enemy ranged attacks only effective within 18"

enhancements.name: Borthrod Gland
enhancements.effect: Kroot Flesh Shaper only. While the bearer is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes a melee attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.

enhancements.name: Kroothawk Flock
enhancements.effect: KROOT model only. Ranged weapons equipped by models in the bearer’s unit have the [IGNORES COVER] ability, and enemy units that are set up on the battlefield as Reinforcements cannot be set up within 12" horizontally of the bearer.

enhancements.name: Nomadic Hunter
enhancements.effect: KROOT TRAIL SHAPER model only. While the bearer is leading a unit, add 3" to the Move characteristic of models in that unit and ranged weapons equipped by models in that unit have the [ASSAULT] ability.

enhancements.name: Root-carved Weapons
enhancements.effect: Kroot War Shaper model only. All weapons equipped by the bearer have the [PRECISION] and [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] abilities.

rules.name: Integrated Command Structure
rules.effect:
**^^Kroot^^** and **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** units from your army have the following ability:

**Targeting Triangulation (Aura):** While an enemy unit is within 9" of and visible to this unit, each time a ranged attack made by a friendly **^^T’au Empire^^** model (excluding **^^Kroot^^**, **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** and TITANIC models) targets that enemy unit, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.

**^^T’au Empire^^** units (excluding **^^Kroot^^** and **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** units) from your army have the following ability:

**Localised Stealth Projectors (Aura):** While a friendly **^^Kroot^^** or **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** unit is wholly within 6" of and visible to this unit, that **^^Kroot^^** or **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18".

stratagems.name: Experimental Modifications
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of weapons equipped by models in your unit by 1.
stratagems.summary: Improve AP by 1 on unit's weapons

stratagems.name: Multisensory Scanning
stratagems.timing: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, re-roll a Wound roll of 1. If it is a KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS unit, you can re-roll the Wound roll instead.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll Wound rolls of 1; KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS re-roll any Wound roll

stratagems.name: Interlocking Manouevres
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that was eligible to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: If your unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Normal move of up to 6". Otherwise, your unit can make a Fall Back move. It cannot embark within a TRANSPORT at the end of this move if it disembarked from a TRANSPORT this turn.
stratagems.summary: Move 6" if not in Engagement Range, or Fall Back; cannot embark if disembarked this turn

stratagems.name: Pheromone Waypoints
stratagems.cond: One KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS unit from your army that has not been selected to move this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time your unit Advances, do not make an Advance roll for it. Instead, until the end of the phase, add 6" to the Move characteristic of models in your unit.
stratagems.summary: Unit Advances without roll; +6" Move until end of phase

stratagems.name: Alien Expertise
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the turn, your unit is eligible to shoot in a turn in which it Advanced. If it is a KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS unit, until the end of the turn, your unit is eligible to declare a charge in a turn in which it Advanced as well.
stratagems.summary: Shoot after Advancing; KROOT/VESPID STINGWINGS also charge after Advancing

stratagems.name: Guided Fire
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army (excluding KROOT and VESPID STINGWINGS units) that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit. If your unit is wholly within 9" of one or more KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS units from your army, add 2 to the Strength characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit instead.
stratagems.summary: +1 Strength ranged weapons; +2 if wholly within 9" of KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS

enhancements.name: Student of Kauyon
enhancements.effect: KROOT SHAPER model only. In the Declare Battle Formations step, you can select up to three KROOT CARNIVORES or KROOT FARSTALKERS units from your army. Models in those units have the Deep Strike ability.

enhancements.name: Admired Leader
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model (excluding KROOT models) only. In your Command phase, you can select one KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS unit within 12" of the bearer. If you do, until the start of your next Command phase, improve the Leadership characteristic of models in that unit by 1, and while such a unit is not Battle-shocked, add 1 to the Objective Control characteristic of models in that unit.

enhancements.name: Fanatical Convert
enhancements.effect: KROOT model only. The bearer’s unit has the For the Greater Good ability.

enhancements.name: Transponder Lock Module
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE WALKER model with the Deep Strike ability only. The bearer’s unit can be set up using the Deep Strike ability in the Reinforcements step of your first, second or third Movement phase, regardless of any mission rules. During your first Movement phase, when you set up the bearer’s unit, it must be set up within 12" of one or more friendly KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS units and not within 9" horizontally of one or more enemy units.

rules.name: Superior Craftmanship
rules.effect: Add 6" to the Range characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by **^^T'au Empire^^** models from your army.

stratagems.name: Automated Repair Drones
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Select one BATTLESUIT model in your unit: that model regains up to D3+1 lost wounds.
stratagems.summary: BATTLESUIT regains up to D3+1 lost wounds

stratagems.name: Reactive Impact Dampeners
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE BATTLESUIT unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time an attack targets your unit, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than the Toughness characteristic of your unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.
stratagems.summary: -1 to Wound rolls when S > T

stratagems.name: Experimental Weaponry
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time you roll one or more dice to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon equipped by a model in your unit, you can re-roll the result.
stratagems.summary: Re-roll attack count dice for unit's weapons

stratagems.name: Experimental Ammunition
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Select one of the following to apply to your unit until the end of the phase: Improve the Strength characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit by 1.Improve the Strength and Armour Penetration characteristics of ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit by 1, and those weapons have the [HAZARDOUS] ability.
stratagems.summary: Select one: +1 Strength to ranged weapons, OR +1 Strength and AP, ranged weapons gain [HAZARDOUS]

stratagems.name: Threat Assessment Analyser
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase.
stratagems.effect: Select the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] or [LETHAL HITS] ability. Until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have the selected ability. You can instead select the [SUSTAINED HITS 1], [LETHAL HITS] and [HAZARDOUS] abilities to apply to those weapons until the end of the phase.
stratagems.summary: Grant [SUSTAINED HITS 1] or [LETHAL HITS] to unit's ranged weapons; or grant all three abilities including [HAZARDOUS]

stratagems.name: Neuroweb System Jammer
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets.
stratagems.cond: One T’AU EMPIRE CRISIS unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18".
stratagems.summary: Enemy ranged attacks only effective within 18"

enhancements.name: Supernova Launcher
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. Select one airbursting fragmentation projector equipped by the bearer. Improve the Strength characteristic of that weapon by 3, and improve the Armour Penetration and Damage characteristics of that weapon by 1.

enhancements.name: Thermoneutronic Projector
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. Select one T’au flamer equipped by the bearer. Improve the Strength characteristic of that weapon by 2, and improve the Armour Penetration and Damage characteristics of that weapon by 1.

enhancements.name: Plasma Accelerator Rifle
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. Select one plasma rifle equipped by the bearer. Improve the Strength characteristic of that weapon by 2, and improve the Attacks, Armour Penetration and Damage characteristics of that weapon by 1.

enhancements.name: Fusion Blades
enhancements.effect: T’AU EMPIRE model only. Select one fusion blaster equipped by the bearer. Improve the Attacks characteristic of that weapon by 1, improve the Strength characteristic of that weapon by 3, and that weapon has the [MELTA 4] ability.

rules.name: Guerrilla Ambushers
rules.effect: Each time an enemy unit is selected to fire Overwatch, roll one D6: on a 4+, until the end of the turn, that unit cannot make Overwatch attacks that target **^^Kroot^^** units from your army.

rules.name: Forming Boarding Squads
rules.effect: In the Form Boarding Squads step, a **^^Kroot Farstalkers^^** unit is split into two Boarding Squads, each containing 6 models.

rules.name: Mustering A Boarding Patrol
rules.effect:
You can include up to two of the following units (duplicates are not allowed):
- **^^Kroot Flesh Shaper^^**
- **^^Kroot Trail Shaper^^**
- **^^Kroot War Shaper^^**

You can include up to three of the following unit:
- **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** (10 models)

For each **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** unit you include, you can include up to one of each of the following units:
- **^^Kroot Farstalkers^^** (12 models)
- **^^Kroot Hounds^^** (5 models)
- **^^Krootox Riders^^** (1 model)

rules.name: Rules Adaptions
rules.effect:
- **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** units can perform the Secure Site Tactical Manoeuvre as if they had the **^^Battleline^^** keyword.
- Each time you set up a **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** unit in an Entry Zone, you can set up a **^^Kroot Hounds^^** unit and/or a **^^Krootox Riders^^** unit in that same Entry Zone.
- If a **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** or **^^Kroot Farstalkers^^** unit from your army destroys an enemy unit while receiving the benefits of the **^^Kroot Flesh Shaper^^**'s Rites of Feasting ability, the Feel No Pain 5+ ability they gain stays in effect, even after they stop receiving the benefit of that ability.
- The **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** unit loses the Fieldcraft ability.
- The **^^Kroot Hounds^^** unit loses the Loping Pounce ability.
- The **^^Krootox Riders^^** unit loses the Kroot Packmates ability.

rules.name: Void Combat Discipline
rules.effect:
Units from your army with the For the Greater Good ability lose that ability and **^^T'au Empire^^** units from your army gain this one.

Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, it can ignore other models in this unit for the purposes of determining visibility. In addition, this unit is eligible to perform the Set Overwatch Tactical Manoeuvre in a turn in which it Advanced or Fell Back.

stratagems.name: Boarding Blades
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of melee weapons equipped by models in your unit by 1. In addition, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, on a Critical Wound, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by an additional 1.
stratagems.summary: +1 AP to melee weapons; +1 AP on Critical Wounds

stratagems.name: Sweeping Ambush
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a Pile-in or Consolidation move, it can move up to 6" instead of up to 3". In addition, when making a Consolidation move, that model can end that move within Engagement Range of the closest enemy unit that was not visible to its unit when it started that move.
stratagems.summary: Pile-in/Consolidation moves up to 6"; Consolidation can reach previously-hidden enemies

stratagems.name: Brute Force
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, just after a KROOTOX RIDERS unit from your army opens a Hatchway.
stratagems.cond: That KROOTOX RIDERS unit.
stratagems.effect: Roll one D6 for each unit that is within 3" of that Hatchway, if that Hatchway is visible to that unit and that unit is wholly on the opposite side of it from your unit: on a 2-4, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound; on a 5+, that enemy unit suffers 2 mortal wounds.
stratagems.summary: Roll D6 per enemy unit within 3" of Hatchway (visible, opposite side): 2-4 = 1 mortal wound, 5+ = 2 mortal wounds

stratagems.name: Tanglebomb Bolas
stratagems.cond: One KROOT unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Select one enemy unit within 9" of and visible to your unit and roll one D6: on a 2+, until the start of your next Shooting phase, halve the Move characteristic of models in that enemy unit and halve Advance and Charge rolls made for it.
stratagems.summary: Roll D6 for enemy unit within 9": on 2+, halve Move, Advance and Charge rolls until your next Shooting phase

rules.name: Rules Adaptions
rules.effect:
- Rules that refer to Observer, Guided and/or Spotted units have no effect.
- **^^Crisis Battlesuits^^** units lose the Deep Strike ability.
- The **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** unit loses the Fieldcraft ability.
- The **^^Stealth Battlesuits^^** unit loses the Homing Beacon ability.
- The **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** unit loses the Deep Strike and Airborne Agility abilities.

rules.name: Mustering A Boarding Patrol
rules.effect:
You can include up to two of the following units (duplicates are not allowed):
- **^^Cadre Fireblade^^**
- **^^Darkstrider^^**
- **^^Ethereal^^**

You can include up to three of each of the following units:
- **^^Breacher Team^^** (10 models)
- **^^Pathfinder Team^^** (5 or 10 models)
- **^^Strike Team^^** (10 models)

You can include up to one of each of the following units:
- **^^Kroot Carnivores^^** (10 models)
- **^^Stealth Battlesuits^^** (3 models)
- **^^Vespid Stingwings^^** (5 models)

You can include up to one of the following units:
- **^^Crisis Fireknife Battlesuits^^** (3 models)
- **^^Crisis Starscythe Battlesuits^^** (3 models)
- **^^Crisis Sunforge Battlesuits^^** (3 models)

rules.name: Forming Boarding Squads
rules.effect: **^^Fire Warrior^^** units that do not contain a Fire Warrior Shas'ui or Breacher Fire Warrior Shas'ui model after you have Boarding Squads lose the **^^Markerlight^^** keyword.

rules.name: Markerlight Precision
rules.effect: Each time a **^^T'au Empire^^** unit from your army (excluding **^^Kroot^^** units) is selected to shoot, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have their Ballistic Skill characteristic improved by 1 and have the **[SUSTAINED HITS 1]** ability while targeting an enemy unit that is visible to one or more friendly **^^Markerlight^^** units that are not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy models.

rules.name: Void Combat Discipline
rules.effect:
Units from your army with the For the Greater Good ability lose that ability and **^^T'au Empire^^** units from your army gain this one.

Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, it can ignore other models in this unit for the purposes of determining visibility. In addition, this unit is eligible to perform the Set Overwatch Tactical Manoeuvre in a turn in which it Advanced or Fell Back.

stratagems.name: Firing Line
stratagems.timing: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit destroys a T’AU EMPIRE unit from your army.
stratagems.cond: One FIRE WARRIOR or PATHFINDER TEAM unit from your army that is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, has not fired Overwatch this turn and is within 9" of that enemy unit.
stratagems.effect: Your unit can shoot as if it were your Shooting phase, but when doing so, models in your unit can only target that enemy unit (and only if it is an eligible target) and, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ is required to score a hit.
stratagems.summary: Shoot outside Shooting phase targeting only one enemy unit; need unmodified 5+ to hit

stratagems.name: Retreating Fire
stratagems.cond: One FIRE WARRIOR or PATHFINDER TEAM unit from your army.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, your unit is eligible to shoot in a turn in which it Fell Back.
stratagems.summary: Unit can shoot after Fall Back this turn

stratagems.name: Responsive Volley
stratagems.timing: Your Movement phase, just after a FIRE WARRIOR or PATHFINDER TEAM unit from your army opens a Hatchway.
stratagems.cond: That FIRE WARRIOR or PATHFINDER TEAM unit.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the turn, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack that targets an enemy unit wholly on the opposite side of that Hatchway from them, a Critical Hit is scored on an unmodified Hit roll of 5+, instead of only a 6.
stratagems.summary: Critical Hits on 5+ for attacks through Hatchways to opposite side

stratagems.name: Pulse Barrage
stratagems.timing: Your opponent’s Movement or Charge phase.
stratagems.cond: One FIRE WARRIOR or PATHFINDER TEAM unit from your army that has not fired Overwatch this phase.
stratagems.effect: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack while firing Overwatch, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ is required to score a hit, instead of an unmodified 6.
stratagems.summary: Overwatch hits on 5+ instead of 6

abilities.name: Infiltrators

abilities.name: Lone Operative
abilities.effect: Unless part of an Attached unit, this unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 12".

abilities.name: Stealth
abilities.effect: If every model in a unit has this ability, then each time a ranged attack is made against it, subtract 1 from that attack’s Hit roll.

abilities.name: For The Greater Good
abilities.effect:
If your Army Faction is **^^T’au Empire^^**, at the start of your Shooting phase you can select units from your army with this ability to become Observer units.

During your Shooting phase, for each Observer unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase and is eligible to shoot (excluding **^^Fortification^^** and Battle‑shocked units) select one enemy unit that is visible to be marked as their Spotted unit until the end of the phase. Each enemy unit can only be marked as a Spotted unit once per phase.

Units from your army with the For the Greater Good ability (excluding Observer units) are Guided units while targeting one or more Spotted units.

Until the end of the phase, each time a model from your army in a Guided unit makes an attack that targets a Spotted unit, improve the Ballistic Skill characteristic of that attack by 1 and, if the Spotted unit was marked by an Observer unit that has the Markerlight keyword, that attack has the **[IGNORES COVER]** ability.

abilities.name: Way of the Short Blade
abilities.effect: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit within 9", add 1 to the Wound roll.

abilities.name: Puretide's Teachings
abilities.effect: Once per battle round, one unit from your army with this ability can use it when its unit is targeted with a Stratagem. If it does, reduce the CP cost of that use of that Stratagem by 1CP.

abilities.effect:

abilities.name: Invulnerable Save (4+)

abilities.name: Deep Strike
abilities.effect:
During the Declare Battle Formations step, if every model in a unit has this ability, you can set it up in Reserves instead of setting it up on the battlefield. If you do, in the Reinforcements step of one of your Movement phases you can set up this unit anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models.

If a unit with the Deep Strike ability arrives from Strategic Reserves, the controlling player can choose for that unit to be set up either using the rules for Strategic Reserves or using the Deep Strike ability.

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.effect:
While a Bodyguard unit contains a Leader, it is known as an Attached unit and, with the exception of rules that are triggered when units are destroyed (pg 12), it is treated as a single unit for all rules purposes. Each time an attack targets an Attached unit, until the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks, you must use the Toughness characteristic of the Bodyguard models in that unit, even if a Leader in that unit has a different Toughness characteristic. Each time an attack successfully wounds an Attached unit, that attack cannot be allocated to a Character model in that unit, even if that Character model has lost one or more wounds or has already had attacks allocated to it this phase. As soon as the last Bodyguard model in an Attached unit has been destroyed, any attacks made against that unit that have yet to be allocated can then be allocated to Character models in that unit.

Each time the last model in a Bodyguard unit is destroyed, each CHARACTER unit that is part of that Attached unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time the last model in a CHARACTER unit that is attached to a Bodyguard unit is destroyed and there is not another CHARACTER unit attached, that Attached unit’s Bodyguard unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time a unit that is part of an Attached unit is destroyed, it does not have the keywords of any other units that make up that Attached unit (unless it has those keywords on its own datasheet) for the purposes of any rules that would be triggered when that unit is destroyed.

Each time an attack made with such a weapon successfully wounds an Attached unit, if a Character model in that unit is visible to the attacking model, the attacking model’s player can choose to have that attack allocated to that Character model instead of following the normal attack sequence.

abilities.name: Lethal Hits
abilities.effect: Weapons with **[LETHAL HITS]** in their profile are known as Lethal Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, a Critical Hit automatically wounds the target.

abilities.name: Rapid Deployment
abilities.effect: Units can disembark from this TRANSPORT after it has Advanced. Units that do so count as having made a Normal move that phase, and cannot declare a charge in the same turn, but can otherwise act normally in the remainder of the turn.

abilities.name: Devilfish
abilities.effect: Capacity: This model has a transport capacity of 12 T’AU EMPIRE INFANTRY models. It cannot transport BATTLESUIT, KROOT or VESPID STINGWINGS models.

abilities.name: Deadly Demise D3
abilities.effect: Some models have 'Deadly Demise x' listed in their abilities. When such a model is destroyed, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a TRANSPORT, roll before any embarked models disembark). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by 'x' (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6").

abilities.name: For The Greater Good
abilities.effect:
If your Army Faction is **^^T’au Empire^^**, at the start of your Shooting phase you can select units from your army with this ability to become Observer units.

During your Shooting phase, for each Observer unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase and is eligible to shoot (excluding **^^Fortification^^** and Battle‑shocked units) select one enemy unit that is visible to be marked as their Spotted unit until the end of the phase. Each enemy unit can only be marked as a Spotted unit once per phase.

Units from your army with the For the Greater Good ability (excluding Observer units) are Guided units while targeting one or more Spotted units.

Until the end of the phase, each time a model from your army in a Guided unit makes an attack that targets a Spotted unit, improve the Ballistic Skill characteristic of that attack by 1 and, if the Spotted unit was marked by an Observer unit that has the Markerlight keyword, that attack has the **[IGNORES COVER]** ability.

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.effect:
While a Bodyguard unit contains a Leader, it is known as an Attached unit and, with the exception of rules that are triggered when units are destroyed (pg 12), it is treated as a single unit for all rules purposes. Each time an attack targets an Attached unit, until the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks, you must use the Toughness characteristic of the Bodyguard models in that unit, even if a Leader in that unit has a different Toughness characteristic. Each time an attack successfully wounds an Attached unit, that attack cannot be allocated to a Character model in that unit, even if that Character model has lost one or more wounds or has already had attacks allocated to it this phase. As soon as the last Bodyguard model in an Attached unit has been destroyed, any attacks made against that unit that have yet to be allocated can then be allocated to Character models in that unit.

Each time the last model in a Bodyguard unit is destroyed, each CHARACTER unit that is part of that Attached unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time the last model in a CHARACTER unit that is attached to a Bodyguard unit is destroyed and there is not another CHARACTER unit attached, that Attached unit’s Bodyguard unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time a unit that is part of an Attached unit is destroyed, it does not have the keywords of any other units that make up that Attached unit (unless it has those keywords on its own datasheet) for the purposes of any rules that would be triggered when that unit is destroyed.

abilities.effect:
If your Army Faction is **^^T’au Empire^^**, at the start of your Shooting phase you can select units from your army with this ability to become Observer units.

Each enemy unit can only be marked as a Spotted unit once per phase.

abilities.name: Scouts 7"
abilities.effect:
Some units have ‘Scouts x"’ listed in their abilities. If every model in a unit has this ability, then at the start of the first battle round, before the first turn begins, it can make a Normal move of up to x", with the exception that, while making that move, the distance moved by each model in that unit can be greater than that model's Move characteristic, as long as it is not greater than x". 

DEDICATED TRANSPORT models can make use of any Scouts x" ability listed in their abilities, or a Scouts x" ability that a unit that starts the battle embarked within that DEDICATED TRANSPORT  model has (provided only models with this ability are embarked within that Dedicated Transport model), regardless of how that embarked unit gained this ability (e.g. listed in their abilities, conferred by an Enhancement or by an attached Character, etc.). 

A unit that moves using this ability must end that move more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models. If both players have units that can do this, the player who is taking the first turn moves their units first.

abilities.name: Stealth
abilities.effect: If every model in a unit has this ability, then each time a ranged attack is made against it, subtract 1 from that attack’s Hit roll.

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.effect:
While a Bodyguard unit contains a Leader, it is known as an Attached unit and, with the exception of rules that are triggered when units are destroyed (pg 12), it is treated as a single unit for all rules purposes. Each time an attack targets an Attached unit, until the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks, you must use the Toughness characteristic of the Bodyguard models in that unit, even if a Leader in that unit has a different Toughness characteristic. Each time an attack successfully wounds an Attached unit, that attack cannot be allocated to a Character model in that unit, even if that Character model has lost one or more wounds or has already had attacks allocated to it this phase. As soon as the last Bodyguard model in an Attached unit has been destroyed, any attacks made against that unit that have yet to be allocated can then be allocated to Character models in that unit.

Each time the last model in a Bodyguard unit is destroyed, each CHARACTER unit that is part of that Attached unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time the last model in a CHARACTER unit that is attached to a Bodyguard unit is destroyed and there is not another CHARACTER unit attached, that Attached unit’s Bodyguard unit becomes a separate unit, with its original Starting Strength. If this happens as the result of an attack, they become separate units after the attacking unit has resolved all of its attacks. 

Each time a unit that is part of an Attached unit is destroyed, it does not have the keywords of any other units that make up that Attached unit (unless it has those keywords on its own datasheet) for the purposes of any rules that would be triggered when that unit is destroyed.

glossary.effect: Weapons with **[ASSAULT]** in their profile are known as Assault weapons. When such a unit is selected to shoot, you can only resolve attacks using Assault weapons its models are equipped with.

glossary.name: Blast
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[BLAST]** in their profile are known as Blast weapons, and they make a random number of attacks. Each time you determine how many attacks are made with a Blast weapon, add 1 to the result for every five models that were in the target unit when you selected it as the target (rounding down). Blast weapons can never be used to make attacks against a unit that is within Engagement Range of one or more units from the attacking model’s army (including its own unit).

glossary.name: Indirect Fire
glossary.effect:
Weapons with **[INDIRECT FIRE]** in their profile are known as Indirect Fire weapons, and attacks can be made with them even if the target is not visible to the attacking model. These attacks can destroy enemy models in a target unit even though none may have been visible to the attacking unit when you selected that target.


If no models in a target unit are visible to the attacking unit when you select that target, then each time a model in the attacking unit makes an attack against that target using an Indirect Fire weapon, subtract 1 from that attack’s Hit roll, an unmodified Hit roll of 1-3 always fails, and the target has the Benefit of Cover against that attack. Weapons with the **[TORRENT]** ability cannot be fired using the **[INDIRECT FIRE]** ability.

glossary.name: Hazardous
glossary.effect:
Weapons with **[HAZARDOUS]** in their profile are known as Hazardous weapons. Each time a unit is selected to shoot or fight, after that unit has resolved all of its attacks, for each Hazardous weapon that targets were selected for when resolving those attacks, that unit must take one Hazardous test. To do so, roll one D6: on a 1, that test is failed. For each failed test you must resolve the following sequence (resolve each failed test one at a time): 

■ If possible, select one model in that unit that has lost one or more wounds and is equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons. 
■ Otherwise, if possible, select one model in that unit (excluding **^^Character^^** models) equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons. 
■ Otherwise, select one **^^Character^^** model in that unit equipped with one or more Hazardous weapons. 

If a model was selected, that unit suffers 3 mortal wounds and when allocating those mortal wounds, they must be allocated to the selected model. 

If a unit from a player’s army is selected as the target of the Fire Overwatch Stratagem in their opponent’s Charge phase, any mortal wounds inflicted by Hazardous tests are allocated after the charging unit has ended its Charge move.

glossary.name: Torrent
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[TORRENT]** in their profile are known as Torrent weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, that attack automatically hits the target.

glossary.name: Ignores Cover
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[IGNORES COVER]** in their profile are known as Ignores Cover weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, the target cannot have the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

glossary.name: Rapid Fire
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[RAPID FIRE X]** in their profile are known as Rapid Fire weapons. Each time such a weapon targets a unit within half that weapon’s range, the Attacks characteristic of that weapon is increased by the amount denoted by ‘x’.

glossary.name: Sustained Hits
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[SUSTAINED HITS X]** in their profile are known as Sustained Hits weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if a Critical Hit is rolled, that attack scores a number of additional hits on the target as denoted by ‘x’

glossary.name: Scouts
glossary.effect:
Some units have ‘Scouts x"’ listed in their abilities. If every model in a unit has this ability, then at the start of the first battle round, before the first turn begins, it can make a Normal move of up to x", with the exception that, while making that move, the distance moved by each model in that unit can be greater than that model's Move characteristic, as long as it is not greater than x". 

DEDICATED TRANSPORT models can make use of any Scouts x" ability listed in their abilities, or a Scouts x" ability that a unit that starts the battle embarked within that DEDICATED TRANSPORT  model has (provided only models with this ability are embarked within that Dedicated Transport model), regardless of how that embarked unit gained this ability (e.g. listed in their abilities, conferred by an Enhancement or by an attached Character, etc.). 

A unit that moves using this ability must end that move more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models. If both players have units that can do this, the player who is taking the first turn moves their units first.

glossary.name: Deadly Demise
glossary.effect: Some models have 'Deadly Demise x' listed in their abilities. When such a model is destroyed, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a TRANSPORT, roll before any embarked models disembark). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by 'x' (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6").

glossary.name: One Shot
glossary.effect: The bearer can only shoot with this weapon once per battle.

glossary.name: Devastating Wounds
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[DEVASTATING WOUNDS]** in their profile are known as Devastating Wounds weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if that attack scores a Critical Wound, no saving throw of any kind can be made against that attack (including invulnerable saving throws). Such attacks are only allocated to models after all other attacks made by the attacking unit have been allocated and resolved. After that attack is allocated and after any modifiers are applied, it inflicts a number of mortal wounds on the target equal to the Damage characteristic of that attack, instead of inflicting damage normally.

glossary.name: Heavy
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[HEAVY]** in their profile are known as Heavy weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if the attacking model’s unit Remained Stationary this turn, add 1 to that attack’s Hit roll.

glossary.name: Extra Attacks
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[EXTRA ATTACKS]** in their profile are known as Extra Attacks weapons. Each time the bearer of one or more Extra Attacks weapons fights, it makes attacks with each of the Extra Attacks melee weapons it is equipped with and it makes attacks with one of the melee weapons it is equipped with that does not have the [EXTRA ATTACKS] ability (if any). The number of attacks made with an Extra Attacks weapon cannot be modified by other rules, unless that weapon’s name is explicitly specified in that rule.

glossary.name: Anti-
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[ANTI-KEYWORD X+]** in their profile are known as Anti weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon against a target with the keyword after the word ‘Anti-’, an unmodified Wound roll of ‘x+’ scores a Critical Wound.

glossary.name: Feel No Pain
glossary.effect: Some models have 'Feel No Pain x+' listed in their abilities. Each time a model with this ability suffers damage and so would lose a wound (including wounds lost due to mortal wounds), roll one D6: if the result is greater than or equal to the number denoted by 'x: that wound is ignored and is not lost. If a model has more than one Feel No Pain ability, you can only use one of those abilities each time that model suffers damage and so would lose a wound.

glossary.name: Firing Deck
glossary.effect: Some **^^Transport^^** models have ‘Firing Deck x’ listed in their abilities. Each time such a model is selected to shoot in the Shooting phase, you can select up to ‘x’ models embarked within it whose units have not already shot this phase. Then, for each of those embarked models, you can select one ranged weapon that embarked model is equipped with (excluding weapons with the **[ONE SHOT]** ability). Until that **^^Transport^^** model has resolved all of its attacks, it counts as being equipped with all of the weapons you selected in this way, in addition to its other weapons. Until the end of the phase, those selected models’ units are not eligible to shoot.

glossary.name: Hover
glossary.effect:
Some **^^Aircraft^^** models have 'Hover' listed in their abilities. When you are instructed to Declare Battle Formations, before doing anything else, you must first declare which models from your army with this ability will be in Hover mode.

If a model is in Hover mode, then until the end of the battle, its Move characteristic is changed to 20", it loses the **^^Aircraft^^** keyword and it loses all associated rules for being an **^^Aircraft^^** model. Models in Hover mode do not start the battle in Reserves, but you can choose to place them into Strategic Reserves following the normal rules if you wish.

glossary.name: Lance
glossary.effect: Weapons with **[LANCE]** in their profile are known as Lance weapons. Each time an attack is made with such a weapon, if the bearer made a Charge move this turn, add 1 to that attack’s Wound roll.

glossary.name: Crucible
glossary.effect: A **^^Character^^** created using Crucible rules has the Crucible ability. Your army may include up to three units with the Crucible ability and these units cannot be given Enhancements.
